name: How often do I need to update my content if I want Google AI Overviews to keep trusting and citing my site?
description: Review and update your key AI Overview–targeted pages every few months. Visible, meaningful updates—new details, refined answers, refreshed FAQs—signal freshnes

mainEntity.name: How often do I need to update my content if I want Google AI Overviews to keep trusting and citing my site?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Regularly updated content has a clear advantage in Google AI Overviews, especially in fast-changing topics. Stale pages are less likely to be trusted as sources for current answers.

Google’s AI looks at freshness signals like updated dates, changing examples, and revised details. Pages that reflect recent realities, new options, or seasonal changes show that you’re actively maintaining accuracy, which matters for AI summarization.

Key factors:
- Visible last-updated dates on key pages
- Periodic content reviews and revisions
- Incorporating new data, examples, or product changes
- Refreshing FAQs as user questions evolve

Practically, set a schedule to review your most important AI Overview–targeted pages every 3–6 months, updating specifics, clarifying answers, and expanding FAQs. Mark the updated date and ensure schema reflects the changes.

Soft positioning: AI Overview inclusion tends to favor sites that treat content as a living asset, not a one-time publication.
acceptedAnswer.description: Review and update your key AI Overview–targeted pages every few months. Visible, meaningful updates—new details, refined answers, refreshed FAQs—signal freshness and make Google’s AI more likely to trust and continue citing your content.
